hero_title: Preserve SAR and STR Audit Trails
hero_subtitle: SFTY structures suspicious activity reviews by linking provider outputs, preserving evidence, and centralizing case management for clear audit history and defensible SAR reporting.

problem_title: Audit readiness weakens across scattered SAR evidence
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to preserve a clear audit trail when SAR and STR evidence remains dispersed across multiple systems and manual processes. This fragmentation slows suspicious activity review and complicates defensible reporting during regulatory audits or internal examinations.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects suspicious activity alerts, reviewer notes, case decisions and regulatory drafts into a unified workflow that preserves a clear, traceable audit trail for SAR and STR preparation.
workflow_title: From alerts to SAR and STR reporting
workflow_description: SFTY routes AML alerts through structured case review, decision logging and evidence preservation to support SAR and STR drafting with a clear audit trail.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for AML compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a connected compliance layer that connects screening, monitoring, case management, and reporting workflows for AML teams at digital asset businesses such as VASPs and CASPs.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support SAR and STR workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures SAR and STR workflows by linking alerts, provider outputs, cases, decision logs and regulatory rules into audit-ready packages for efficient suspicious activity reporting.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves evidence history, decision logs, and case records with role-based access and audit logging to support review readiness and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY sits above existing KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, connecting their outputs into structured workflows without replacing any underlying compliance tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC, KYT or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace providers but connects their outputs into one controlled compliance operating layer, enabling holistic review and audit-ready reporting.
final_title: Preserve audit-ready SAR and STR evidence
final_description: Review how SFTY structures case workflows, links provider data, and supports traceable audit history for SAR and STR compliance.
